skill.name: github-release-management
skill.description: Automates GitHub release planning, changelog generation, validation, publishing, hotfixes, and rollback workflows for software teams.
skill.summary: Coordinate GitHub releases from version planning through publishing, validation, and rollback.
skill.author: Claude Flow Team

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and token-handling patterns in SKILL.md. Review confirmed these are mostly release-management examples, but the skill can guide agents to publish packages, create releases, write token-backed npm configuration, and run CI deployment commands. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found, so publication is acceptable with a clear operational-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Powerful Release Automation Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md contains many examples that run GitHub CLI, git, npm, Docker, and claude-flow commands. This is expected for release management, but misuse could create releases, publish packages, push images, or alter repository state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and perform privileged release actions. Context shows legitimate release intent, so this is operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: CI Secret Use During Package Publishing
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The workflow authenticates GitHub CLI with GITHUB_TOKEN and writes NPM_TOKEN into .npmrc before publishing. This is common CI practice, but it handles credentials and can expose tokens if logs, permissions, or cleanup are misconfigured.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential handling is explicit and tied to package publishing. It appears legitimate, but it increases risk because release tokens are high-impact secrets.

medium_findings.title: Temporary Release Data Files
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The workflow writes release PR and commit data into /tmp files and reads them into changelog generation. This is not secret exfiltration, but shared temporary paths can cause data leakage or collision in weaker CI isolation models.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The /tmp usage is visible and used for release metadata. The data appears low sensitivity, so the concern is moderate and environment-dependent.

low_findings.title: Static Network URL Matches Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Hardcoded URLs point to GitHub CLI, Semantic Versioning, Claude Flow documentation, issues, and discussions. No evidence found that these URLs receive secrets or release data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or registry references. I found no network exfiltration code or suspicious external endpoint in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misread As Path Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The ../ sequences are Markdown links to local documentation files under Related Resources. No file-read or file-write operation uses these paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged paths are Markdown references, not executable filesystem operations. Their context is a documentation list.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Static Matches Are Benign Context
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The compliance standards text and major-version migration guidance triggered blocker keywords, but they describe normal release compliance and documentation practices. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior or weak cryptography use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms appear in ordinary release-management prose. Reviewed context does not show malicious networking, cryptographic code, or obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Release Commands With External Side Effects
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to run commands that create releases, branches, pull requests, package publishes, Docker pushes, and rollback operations. Users should require confirmation before production-impacting actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands have direct repository, registry, and deployment side effects. This is core functionality, but it needs operator controls.

dangerous_patterns.title: Token-Backed CI Authentication
dangerous_patterns.description: The GitHub Actions example uses repository and npm secrets for release automation. Tokens should be scoped narrowly, masked, rotated, and used only in protected release environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token usage is explicit and normal for CI publishing. The risk depends on repository permissions and workflow protections.

content.value_statement: Release work often spans versioning, changelogs, testing, publishing, and rollback planning. This skill provides structured GitHub release workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Creates GitHub release drafts and release pull requests with GitHub CLI examples.
Generates changelog content from merged pull requests and commit history.
Coordinates version bumps, release branches, validation, and artifact builds.
Shows GitHub Actions workflows for releases, publishing, security scans, and hotfixes.
Supports npm, Docker, GitHub release assets, staged rollout, and rollback examples.
Uses claude-flow swarm patterns for multi-agent release planning and monitoring.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI, git, Node.js, npm or yarn, and optional claude-flow tooling.
Assumes repository permissions, package registry access, and valid CI secrets.
Examples must be adapted for each repository, branch model, and release policy.
Production publishing and rollback actions still require human approval.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Standard Release
use_cases.description: Plan a version bump, gather changes, create a release branch, and open a release pull request.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Enterprise Deployment
use_cases.description: Run validation, build artifacts, publish packages, monitor rollout health, and prepare rollback steps.

use_cases.title: Handle Emergency Hotfixes
use_cases.description: Create a hotfix branch, run focused tests, publish a patch release, and notify stakeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Release Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a GitHub release for version [version]. Include version files, changelog steps, validation, and manual approval points.

prompt_templates.title: Create Release Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to generate release notes from merged pull requests and commits since [previous tag]. Group changes by feature, fix, security, and breaking change.

prompt_templates.title: Build a CI Release Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a GitHub Actions release workflow for [repository]. Include tests, security checks, artifact upload, package publishing, and protected secrets.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Multi-Repository Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to coordinate releases across [repositories]. Include dependency compatibility, synchronized versions, deployment order, monitoring, and rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: Plan release v2.0.0 for a Node package.
output_examples.output: A release checklist with version bump steps, changelog inputs, validation commands, draft release creation, approval gates, and publish timing.

output_examples.input: Prepare an emergency hotfix for a critical issue.
output_examples.output: A focused hotfix workflow covering branch creation, cherry-pick validation, critical tests, patch release publication, and stakeholder notification.

output_examples.input: Design a rollout with rollback protection.
output_examples.output: A staged deployment plan with canary checks, health metrics, rollback triggers, release monitoring, and post-release validation tasks.

best_practices: Use protected branches, protected environments, and manual approvals for production releases.
Scope GitHub and registry tokens to the minimum permissions required for publishing.
Run tests, security scans, and release-note review before publishing artifacts.
anti_patterns: Do not publish packages directly from an unreviewed agent-generated workflow.
Do not store long-lived tokens in repository files or unchecked workflow output.
Do not skip rollback planning for releases that affect production users.

faq.question: Does this skill create releases automatically?
faq.answer: It provides commands and workflows that can create releases. Users should approve production-impacting commands before execution.

faq.question: Which tools are required?
faq.answer: The skill expects GitHub CLI, git, Node.js, npm or yarn, and optionally claude-flow or swarm tooling.

faq.question: Can it generate changelogs?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows workflows that collect pull requests and commits, then organize release notes by change type.

faq.question: Can it publish npm or Docker artifacts?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples include npm publish and Docker image push steps, which require valid registry credentials.

faq.question: Is it safe for production use?
faq.answer: It is suitable with safeguards. Use scoped tokens, protected environments, review gates, and explicit approval for publish or rollback actions.

faq.question: Does it support emergency hotfixes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes hotfix branch, focused testing, emergency release, and notification workflow examples.
